title: Monetary Policy Implementation - Federal Reserve Bank of New York

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: This source explains the Federal Reserve's monetary policy implementation framework, detailing how the FOMC sets policy stance through the federal funds rate target range and how the New York Fed's Open Market Trading Desk executes operations. The framework relies on administered rates (IORB, ON RRP, SRP) to control short-term rates in an ample-reserves regime, supplemented by standing liquidity facilities including the discount window, FIMA Repo Facility, and central bank swap lines. Asset purchases and balance sheet management serve distinct functions: reserves management, policy accommodation, and market functioning support.
key_points: The federal funds rate is controlled primarily through IORB (interest on reserve balances), which sets a floor under overnight lending rates
ON RRP (Overnight Reverse Repo) provides a floor under money market rates for ineligible entities; SRP (Standing Repo) provides a ceiling
The FOMC conducts eight meetings per year and announces target range adjustments; the Effective Federal Funds Rate (EFFR) is published daily as a volume-weighted median
Asset purchases serve three distinct purposes: reserves management, policy accommodation (easing financial conditions), and market functioning support
Between June 2022 and November 2025, the FOMC directed balance sheet reduction through limits on reinvestment of maturing securities
Standing liquidity facilities (discount window, FIMA Repo, currency swaps) provide backup funding channels during market stress
why_it_matters: Understanding the Fed's implementation framework is essential for market structure analysis because it describes the mechanical plumbing of monetary policy transmission. The interactions between IORB, ON RRP, and SRP create the corridor within which short-term rates fluctuate, directly affecting repo markets, money market funds, and bank funding costs. This framework also clarifies how balance sheet operations (QE, QT) function as distinct tools from rate policy.
macro_context: The source describes the foundational mechanisms through which FOMC decisions transmit to financial markets, but itself contains no current macro catalysts, policy announcements, or forward guidance. It is institutional documentation of the framework.
market_structure_context: Directly relevant to market structure: describes the mechanics of interest rate control in the fed funds market, overnight repo market operations, standing facilities that affect money market structure, and the role of the Desk's operations in maintaining rate targets. The source explains how reserve balances, administered rates, and asset purchases interact to implement policy.
